summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) held a public hearing to address several zoning matters including a rezoning request for a property at 6823 Goshen Road, a variance request (BZA 22-021) for a property at 1330 Ansel Road, and a finding of fact and conclusion of law regarding a school project conditional use request (BZA 2022-022) at 6696 Goshen Road. Public comments were heard, particularly from residents concerned about safety and notification issues related to the school district's plan to lease space for business use. Multiple resolutions were voted on and approved by the board.
decisions: Resolution regarding rezoning of 6823 Goshen Road (parcel 2207I272) approved
Resolution approving variance request BZA 22-021 for 1330 Ansel Road approved
Finding of fact and conclusion of law for BZA 2022-022 (school project conditional use at 6696 Goshen Road) approved
action_items: Board to consider public safety concerns raised about the school district leasing space for business use
key_amounts: $155,000 referenced in connection with land notes and HUD law requirements for properties
notable_topics: Variance request BZA 22-021 for 1330 Ansel Road
School project conditional use request BZA 2022-022 at 6696 Goshen Road
Public concerns about school district leasing building space to businesses
Concerns about inadequate public notification of the school project zoning matter
Safety concerns regarding students accessing off-site leased facilities
notes: This appears to be a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting rather than a regular Board of Trustees meeting. The transcript is heavily degraded with many repeated automated captions and garbled text, making full extraction difficult. A resident (Patty Meyer) expressed strong opposition to the school district's conditional use request, citing lack of public notification, safety concerns for students, and frustration that the decision appeared predetermined. She noted HealthSource (the prospective tenant) was not even present at the hearing. The board acknowledged the concerns but noted much of the discussion had occurred at the prior September 29, 2022 special meeting.
